_comment_minimum_wage: Daily minimum wage in pesos. CONASAMI raised these on Jan 1, 2026. 'general' applies to most of Mexico; 'northern_border' applies to the 45 municipalities in the Zona Libre de la Frontera Norte (ZLFN).
_comment_uma: UMA = Unidad de Medida y Actualizacion. Used as the reference for IMSS/INFONAVIT contribution caps and ceilings. INEGI updates this every February 1st. The 2026 value (117.31) takes effect Feb 1, 2026; for January 2026, use the 2025 UMA value (113.14) — see uma_daily_value_jan_override below for that transition period.
_comment_sbc: SBC = Salario Base de Cotizacion (Contribution Base Salary). The integration factor converts the daily wage into the SBC by adding a proportional share of mandatory benefits (aguinaldo, prima vacacional). This factor assumes the legal minimums (15 days aguinaldo, 25% prima vacacional) and 12 vacation days (1st year). For workers with more vacation days due to seniority, the calculator recomputes a worker-specific factor instead of using this default.

sbc_integration_factor_basis._comment: How 1.0493 is derived for a first-year worker (LFT Art. 84, IMSS Art. 27 LSS): 1 + (aguinaldo_days + vacation_days * prima_vacacional_pct) / 365

_comment_imss: IMSS = Instituto Mexicano del Seguro Social. Each branch ('ramo') has separate employer and worker contribution rates, expressed as a percentage of the SBC (or, for some branches, the excess of SBC over 3x UMA). Domestic workers were incorporated into the mandatory IMSS regime starting with a pilot program; rates below mirror the general regime structure used in IMSS's domestic worker pilot (Programa de Afiliacion de Personas Trabajadoras del Hogar).

enfermedad_maternidad._comment: Sickness & Maternity. 'cuota_fija' is a flat employer fee per UMA. 'excedente' applies only to SBC above 3x UMA, split between employer and worker. 'prestaciones_dinero' and 'gastos_medicos_pensionados' are additional sub-branches.

cesantia_vejez._comment: Old Age & Unemployment in Old Age (RCV - CV). Rate is progressive based on SBC relative to UMA; this is the base rate for SBC up to 1 UMA.

riesgos_trabajo._comment: Occupational Risk Insurance (RT). Domestic work is generally classified as Clase I (lowest risk). Employer-only.

_comment_infonavit: INFONAVIT = Instituto del Fondo Nacional de la Vivienda para los Trabajadores. Housing fund contribution, employer-only, calculated on SBC.
_comment_ceav: CEAV = Cuota de Empleo y Vivienda — the simplified, progressive contribution scheme for domestic workers during 2024-2030 transition. Adjust annually.
_comment_vacation: Vacation days by completed years of service, per LFT Art. 76 as reformed by the 'Vacaciones Dignas' decree (DOF 27-Dec-2022, effective Jan 1, 2023).

vacation_accrual_rule._comment: For years_of_service beyond the last table entry, add 2 days for every additional 5 completed years past year 20.

_comment_aguinaldo: Aguinaldo (Christmas bonus), LFT Art. 87. Legal minimum is 15 days of salary, payable by Dec 20 each year, prorated for partial years of service.
_comment_prima_vacacional: Prima Vacacional (vacation premium), LFT Art. 80. Minimum 25% of the salary corresponding to the vacation days taken.
_comment_seniority_premium_cap: Prima de antiguedad (LFT Art. 162) is capped at twice the general minimum daily wage.
_comment_isr: ISR = Impuesto Sobre la Renta (income tax). The employer withholds ISR from the worker's pay each period and remits it to SAT. Two tables: (1) monthly ISR tariff (Art. 96 LISR) and (2) Subsidio para el Empleo (Art. 113-B LISR, Anexo 8 RMF). Net ISR = max(0, tariff_result - subsidy). NOTE: Values below are 2025 SAT tables. Update to 2026 DOF values when published.

isr_employment_subsidy_monthly._comment: Workers earning up to $11,492.66/month receive the full flat subsidy
Workers earning above the ceiling receive no subsidy

_comment_holidays: Official mandatory rest days for 2026 per LFT Art. 74. 'triple_pay' reflects Art. 75: if a worker works on one of these days, they are owed their regular daily pay (already covered by the weekly/monthly salary) PLUS double pay for the work performed, i.e. triple total for that day.

mandatory_holidays_2026.name: Dia de la Constitucion (observed, 1st Monday of Feb)
Natalicio de Benito Juarez (observed, 3rd Monday of Mar)
Dia de la Revolucion (observed, 3rd Monday of Nov)

_comment_isr_subsidy: Subsidio para el empleo 2026 — DOF 31-Dec-2025. Flat credit of 15.02% × UMA mensual ($3,566.22) = $535.65/month for workers with monthly income up to $11,492.66. Replaces the pre-2024 multi-bracket table. January 2026 technically uses 15.59% × UMA_jan ($3,439.46) = $536.21 — difference of $0.56 is immaterial; single rate used for simplicity.
